macro_segments.title: The Dispute Between Rava and Abaye on Splitting the Effect of a Single Document for Slave Manumission and Property Transfer

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Case: A Get Transferring Both Property and Freeing a Slave
Rava's Position: The Effect of a Single Statement Can Be Split
Abaye's Position: All or Nothing—The Effect Cannot Be Partially Applied

micro_segments.title: Rav Adda's Challenge: Is Rava Following Rabbi Shimon's Principle?

Rabbi Shimon's View: Freedom Occurs Unless an Abstract Percentage is Withheld

macro_segments.title: The Ruling Against Palginan Dibura: Following Rabbi Meir, Not Rabbi Shimon

micro_segments.title: Rav Yosef's Citation: The Halacha Follows Rabbi Meir, Not Rabbi Shimon

The Gemara's Response: Despite Praise, We Rule Like Rabbi Meir

macro_segments.title: The Case of Shachiv Mera (Deathbed Gift) as Evidence for Palginan Dibura

micro_segments.title: Rav Yosef's Case: A Dying Man Gives All Property to His Slave and Then Recovers

macro_segments.title: Distinguishing Palginan Dibura from Qualified Language: The Lav Krisus Principle

micro_segments.title: Abaye's Resolution: When Palginan Dibura Applies vs. When It Does Not
The Key Distinction: Unqualified Text with Split Effect vs. Qualified Text with Reservation
The Principle of Lav Krisus: A Get Must Sever Completely Without Internal Qualification

macro_segments.title: Validation of Signatures When a Get Is Challenged (Irur)

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah: When a Get from Chutz La'aretz Is Challenged, Signatures Must Be Validated
How Many Challengers Are Needed: From One Person to Two to the Husband Himself
The Gemara's Resolution: The Husband's Challenge Demands Validation; Validation Overcomes His Objection

micro_segments.title: The Case Interpretation: Deafness or Muteness as Physical Inability

micro_segments.title: First Similarity: Both Require Befanai Nechtam When Brought In and Out of Eretz Yisrael

Rashi's Explanation: Dina D'Malchuta and Sheva Mitzvot B'nei Noach
